title: Creating a 3D Layered Mandala: Part 6 - Cutting the SVG on a Silhouette Cameo 4
description:
Welcome to the grand finale of our 3D Layered Mandala series. 

We've journeyed together from the initial digital sketch all the way through to a full 3D render, and now, it's time to bring our creation into the physical realm. 

In this final episode, I'll be using the Silhouette Cameo 4 and cardstock to cut out this intricate mandala design. Watch as we assemble each layer, gradually building up to the final, tangible product.

Eager to start creating on your own? You can purchase the SVG cut file from our shop https://designbundles.net/pixaroma/2654600-decorative-mandala-3d-layered-svg-mandala-decor-wa  and get started with your laser machine or paper cutting machines. 

If you've enjoyed this series, please like, subscribe, and share this content to support our future video creation. Thank you for joining us on this creative adventure!

Playlist with entire series here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NWzZCUjsv9I&list=PL-pohOSaL8P_yzx3y9QchBb1chfzFXObV
transcript: Hello fellow designers and crafters.  We've reached the final part of our series, creating a 3D layered mandala.  In this video, you'll witness the exciting process of cutting the SVG file using a silhouette  cameo for cutting machine.  Afterward, I'll demonstrate how to assemble the layered pieces using foam dots.  By following along, you'll gain valuable insights into the practical application of the mandala design.  Cutting the design with precision and utilizing foam dots for assembly ads depth and dimension  to the final piece. So, let's dive in and witness the transformation of our digital creation into  a tangible and visually striking 3D layered mandala. Don't forget to like and subscribe for more  crafting adventures. Now, let's get started and create something extraordinary.  His second layer of our 3D layered mandala is not only the most intricate but also the focal  point of the design. As a result, it requires more time to cut. To enhance its visual appeal and  make it even more captivating, I've chosen to use glitter cardstock. The shimmer and sparkle of  the glitter cardstock adds an extra touch of elegance and interest to this central piece.  This choice ensures that the second layer stands out and becomes a stunning centerpiece of the  overall mandala. While the cutting process may be more time consuming, the end result is well  worth the effort. The intricate details and the added allure of the glitter cardstock make this  layer a true showstopper. While some individuals find the process of weeding to be relaxing and  therapeutic, others may not share the same sentiment. If you are someone who finds solace in this  activity, I encourage you to savor the calming and meditative nature of weeding as you work on  your 3D layered mandala. On the other hand, if weeding isn't your cup of tea, that's perfectly  alright. Crafting is a diverse and multifaceted hobby, and everyone has their own preferences and  enjoyments. It's wonderful that we can embrace different aspects of crafting that bring us joy and  fulfillment. For the second layers of my 3D layered mandala, I've decided to use Dota pattern  cardstock. Crafting allows us to explore different materials and exercise our creativity.  While it's uncertain whether the patterns and lines on the cardstock will enhance the mandala,  I wanted to give it a try since I had the cardstock on hand. In my experience,  when the mandala design itself is complex, using simple cardstock or glitter can provide a  beautiful contrast. On the other hand, when the design itself is simple, opting for pattern  cardstock or floral prints can add an extra layer of visual interest. Crafting offers endless  possibilities for experimentation and personalization. It's all about exploring different combinations  and seeing what works best for each individual project. Embrace your creativity and have fun  playing around with various materials and patterns.  Crafting allows us to explore different materials and have fun playing around with various materials  and have fun playing around with various materials and patterns.
